# host_macos_defaults (Resource)

Manages multiple macOS `defaults` keys as one grouped Terraform resource.

Use this resource when you want one compact macOS preferences block. Each entry in `defaults` still maps to one macOS preference identified by `domain`, `key`, and `current_host`. The map key is only the Terraform-local name for that entry.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_macos_defaults" "settings" {
  defaults = {
    dock_autohide = {
      domain = "com.apple.dock"
      key    = "autohide"
      bool   = true
    }

    dock_hide_recent_apps = {
      domain = "com.apple.dock"
      key    = "show-recents"
      bool   = false
    }

    trackpad_tap_to_click = {
      domain = "com.apple.AppleMultitouchTrackpad"
      key    = "Clicking"
      bool   = true
    }

    languages = {
      domain      = "NSGlobalDomain"
      key         = "AppleLanguages"
      string_list = ["ko-KR", "en-US"]
    }

    show_battery_percent = {
      domain  = "com.apple.menuextra.battery"
      key     = "ShowPercent"
      string  = "YES"
      restart = ["SystemUIServer"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Value Attributes

Each defaults entry must set exactly one of `bool`, `int`, `float`, `string`, or `string_list`.

## Restart Behavior

The provider writes all configured defaults, collects restart processes, de-duplicates them, and restarts each process once. When `restart` is omitted for an entry, the provider applies built-in restarts for known domains such as Dock, Finder, SystemUIServer menu extras, global preferences, trackpad preferences, and accessibility preferences. Set `restart = []` on an entry to disable restarts for that default.

## Destroy Behavior

Removing an entry from `defaults` or destroying the resource leaves that macOS setting in place by default. Set `delete_on_destroy = true` on an entry when Terraform should delete that defaults key after the entry is removed or the grouped resource is destroyed.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `defaults` (Attributes Map) Named macOS defaults to manage. Map keys are Terraform-local names; each object is identified on macOS by `domain`, `key`, and `current_host`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--defaults))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier, always `macos_defaults`.

<a id="nestedatt--defaults"></a>
### Nested Schema for `defaults`

Required:

- `domain` (String) Defaults domain, such as `com.apple.dock`, `NSGlobalDomain`, or `com.apple.universalaccess`.
- `key` (String) Defaults key to manage inside the domain.

Optional:

- `bool` (Boolean) Boolean value. Exactly one of `bool`, `int`, `float`, `string`, or `string_list` must be set.
- `current_host` (Boolean) Use `defaults -currentHost` for host-specific preferences.
- `delete_on_destroy` (Boolean) Delete this defaults key on destroy. Defaults to false, leaving the current host setting in place.
- `float` (Number) Floating-point value. Exactly one of `bool`, `int`, `float`, `string`, or `string_list` must be set.
- `int` (Number) Integer value. Exactly one of `bool`, `int`, `float`, `string`, or `string_list` must be set.
- `restart` (List of String) Process names to restart with `killall` after writes or deletes, such as `Dock`, `Finder`, or `SystemUIServer`. Omit to use provider defaults for known domains; set `[]` to disable restarts.
- `string` (String) String value. Exactly one of `bool`, `int`, `float`, `string`, or `string_list` must be set.
- `string_list` (List of String) String array value. Exactly one of `bool`, `int`, `float`, `string`, or `string_list` must be set.
